what_it_does: Declares a generated surface specification for view B4, a 'Presence dial — 8 modes' UI surface tied to journey J9; it lists the concrete ui:// addresses it builds to, the components involved, and the build/validation artifacts.
observations: A generated surface-spec for view B4 in area B, titled 'Presence dial — 8 modes'.
Home journey is J9 and journeys contains only J9; cross_journeys is empty.
Lists nine concrete ui:// addresses across chat, settings, studio, and toolbar scopes.
build_to names components chat, settings, studio, toolbar; a design_lint command; and a tokens-to-CSS pipeline.
The _what field states the file was built by a Claude Code session and warns never to invent an address.
standouts: The surface is a 'Presence dial' with eight modes, represented by 'RHM-modes'.
It maps one conceptual view to nine distinct UI addresses, including several toolbar/presence sub-paths.
cross_journeys is empty despite the web metaphor in _what, so the surface is journey-local to J9.
conventions: Uses a structured JSON schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, build_to.
Addresses use a ui:// scheme with component/path segments.
Directory path follows a journey spine (tree) layout: surfaces/J9/B4.json.
References external build artifacts and token sources rather than embedding design details.
concerns: The _what field mixes procedural metadata (generated by Claude Code) with semantic description.
The '8 modes' in the title are not enumerated in this file; only the address list hints at some modes (deep-work, no-self-approve, watch-and-react).
build_to.design_lint contains a shell-style command string annotated with '(the FORM gate)', which may be human-readable rather than machine-runnable.
notes: This is a journey-local surface spec that records where a presence-mode dial appears across chat, settings, studio, and toolbar, plus how it should be built and linted.
questions: Where is the corresponding B3 web/address definition that these addresses carry?
Which eight modes exist, and how do they map to the listed addresses?
What does 'RHM-modes' stand for and where is its canonical definition?
Is the design_lint command literal or a template to be expanded by the build system?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a generated surface-spec for view B4 that maps journeys/address-refs to UI addresses without inventing them; the file does exactly that, listing addresses and build targets.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to establish a single source of truth for which UI addresses and components implement the 'Presence dial' surface, keeping the spec tied to a real journey spine and preventing hallucinated addresses.
novelty: It treats a UI 'surface' as a cross-component address map rather than a single screen or component, and it records both the conceptual journey spine location and the concrete build targets.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, address-level mapping for one presence-mode surface, including platform coverage, journey affiliation, and pointers to lint and token artifacts, which a fused system could use to locate and validate related UI implementations.
summary_for_embedding: A generated surface specification for view B4, 'Presence dial — 8 modes', journey J9, listing ui:// addresses across chat, settings, studio, and toolbar and pointing to build components, lint command, and token source.
intention_for_embedding: To record the exact UI addresses and build targets that realize the presence-dial surface for journey J9 without inventing addresses.
